title: Environmental Health and Safety
description: .btn-nyu:hover img { filter: brightness(1.2); opacity: 0.7; transition: all 0.3s ease; } NYU Shanghai is dedicated to pro
text:
Environmental Health and Safety
NYU Shanghai is dedicated to providing all university community members with a safe and healthy environment by prioritizing fire safety, lab safety, occupational health, and environmental protection.
The EHS team provides EHS support, training, consulting, and auditing services in teaching, research, and campus operations. The team also manages risks, promotes sustainability, and implements practices that minimize environmental impact. It ensures that all activities comply with regulations and support the long-term health of both the community and the planet.
Lab Safety Committee
NYU Shanghai Laboratory Safety Committee consists of Campus Safety, Laboratory operation, and the Office of Research. It aims to strengthen the safety management of university laboratories and ensure the safety and compliance of laboratory activities.
Lab Safety Committee Charter
The Composition, Responsibilities, and Work Plan of the Lab Safety Committee
Semi-annual Newsletter
The Semi-annual Newsletter from the Lab Safety Committee Meeting
Lab Safety Newsletter_2025 Fall
Standard Operation Procedure
NYU Shanghai EHS team has developed various guidelines and standard operation procedures as follows. [Log-in Required]
Service
EHS Training
Training courses and matrix of EHS Program
Emergency Response
The emergency preparedness and emergency reponse
Lab Supplies
EHS provides the proactive direction and standard operation procedures (SOP) to ensure the compliance of chemical, biological agent, drugs, microorganism, and equipment, etc., throughout the lifecycle of the acquisition, storage, usage, inventory, and disposal.
For lab supplies procurement, please submit your request via one of the following systems. ChemLife only accepts acquisitions with amounts less than RMB5,000.
Wastes Disposal
Make sure no hazardous materials are placed into regular waste containers, flushed down the drain, or otherwise inappropriately disposed of. If there are any questions, please contact the EHS team.
Hazardous Wastes
BORDER DO NOT TOUCH
SOP of Hazardous Wastes
Medical Wastes
BORDER DO NOT TOUCH
SOP of Medical Wastes
Contact Us
Campus Safety
Service Line (24/7): + 86 21 2059 5500 Email: shanghai.safety@nyu.edu Address: E118, 1F East Hall, the New Bund Campus, 567 Yangsi W Rd.
